steps.title: Rearrange the inequality
steps.content:
$5x - 8y \gt 40 \implies -8y \gt -5x + 40 \implies y \lt \dfrac{5}{8}x - 5$.
The solution set is all points below the line $y = \frac{5}{8}x - 5$ (y-intercept $-5$, x-intercept $8$, positive slope).

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \gt 0$: When $x \lt 0$, the boundary line gives $y = \frac{5}{8}x - 5 \lt -5$. Every point in this region has $y \gt 0 \gt $ (line value), so all points lie above the line. None satisfy $y \lt \frac{5}{8}x - 5$. ✓
  • Choice B — The region where $x \gt 0$ and $y \lt 0$: Test $(9, -1)$: $5(9) - 8(-1) = 45 + 8 = 53 \gt 40$. Contains solutions. ✗
  • Choice C — The region where $x \gt 0$ and $y \gt 0$: Test $(100, 1)$: $500 - 8 = 492 \gt 40$. Contains solutions. ✗
  • Choice D — The $x$-axis: On the x-axis $y = 0$, the inequality becomes $5x \gt 40 \implies x \gt 8$. Points like $(9, 0)$ lie on the x-axis and satisfy it. ✗
A is correct.
